behavior: Diurnal and territorial; forages mainly on the ground with short runs and hops, searching soil and leaf litter for earthworms and other invertebrates while also eating fruit and seeds. Often sings from an elevated perch. Populations may be resident or seasonally migratory.
field_marks: Adult male glossy black overall, including wings and long tail
Bright orange-yellow bill and narrow yellow eye-ring on adult male
Female sooty brown with a paler throat and lightly mottled breast
Rounded, compact thrush body with a relatively long tail
habitat: Woodland and woodland edges with dense undergrowth, as well as hedgerows, scrub, farmland, parks, and gardens; commonly uses open ground beside dense cover for feeding.
